title: Hadrolaelia jongheana
taxonomy: Hadrolaelia jongheana is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This species belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae, a large and complex group of orchids. Within the subfamily, it is placed in the tribe Cymbidieae, a group of orchids known for their showy flowers. Further classification within the tribe places Hadrolaelia jongheana in the subtribe Zygopetalinae, a group of orchids characterized by their fragrant flowers. Its genus, Hadrolaelia, is a small group of terrestrial orchids native to South America.

intro: Hadrolaelia jongheana, also known as the 'Jonghe's Hadrolaelia', is a species of orchid native to Brazil, characterized by its unique, tubular flowers. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections.
